operating_state: Total net sales increased 8.6% to $155.7 billion driven by AWS growth of 16.9% and North America growth of 7.6%, while International growth of 4.9% faced foreign exchange headwinds. Consolidated operating income rose 20.2% to $18.4 billion with operating margin expanding to 11.8%, reflecting improved operational leverage across all segments.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 7% with cost of sales rising 6% and fulfillment costs growing 10% from increased sales and network investments. Technology and infrastructure expenses increased 13% primarily from AWS infrastructure investments, including depreciation increases from shortened server and networking equipment useful lives from six to five years effective January 2025. Stock-based compensation decreased to $3.7 billion from $5.0 billion.
material_events: Converted portion of Anthropic convertible notes to nonvoting preferred stock, recognizing $3.3 billion gain in other income during Q1 2025. Increased commercial paper program capacity to $30.0 billion in April 2025 and committed to invest additional $2.7 billion in Anthropic convertible notes by Q4 2025. Cash capital expenditures increased significantly to $24.3 billion from $13.9 billion primarily for technology infrastructure supporting AWS growth.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange volatility intensified with $1.4 billion unfavorable revenue impact and potential losses of $930 million to $3.7 billion on foreign currency holdings under adverse scenarios. Regulatory proceedings increased across antitrust, privacy, and consumer protection matters with active Federal Trade Commission litigation. Tax exposure expanded through ongoing disputes across multiple jurisdictions including Luxembourg and Indian assessments.
forward_outlook: AWS performance obligations for contracts exceeding one year totaled approximately $189 billion with weighted-average remaining contract life of 4.1 years as of March 31, 2025. Management expects continued increases in cash capital expenditures during 2025 primarily driven by technology infrastructure investments and artificial intelligence capabilities development including commercial arrangement with Anthropic.
uncertainty_profile: Income tax contingencies of $6.3 billion reflect ongoing IRS examinations for 2016 and thereafter plus disputes across multiple jurisdictions with resolution timing highly uncertain. Luxembourg Administrative Court dismissed appeal of €746 million GDPR fine in March 2025 with appeal to higher court filed in April 2025. Legal proceedings outcomes remain unpredictable with potential for substantial future payments or operational restrictions.
